title: Estimate elapsed-time pit-window margins to nearby cars
summary: Pit calls depend on whether a driver can rejoin into clean air or avoid losing position, not only on tyre age.
rationale: Pit calls depend on whether a driver can rejoin into clean air or avoid losing position, not only on tyre age. Public F1 strategy explanations emphasize pit-loss, undercut/overcut, and gap management as central to stop timing.
implementation_hint: Sort by Race-Year, Driver, LapNumber; cumulatively sum cleaned LapTime to approximate elapsed race time. For each current lap, compute gap_to_ahead, gap_to_behind, estimated_rejoin_position_after_pit_loss, clean_air_margin, and undercut_margin_to_ahead using only rows up to the current lap.
expected_effect: Improves discrimination among drivers with similar tyre state but different strategic incentives to pit now.
risk: LapTime outliers and missing safety-car context can distort cumulative gaps; validate with robust clipped lap times and compare with/without wet-weather rows.
